prose:
Method provenance
Property: Frontier orbital energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting), as requested
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied formaldehyde geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz); no relaxation performed (notation: DFT//input)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF 2.x via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill (engine subcommand `frontier`)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings reported by engine. Integrity checks status: ok, trustworthy=true. (No warnings.)
Live log: the run was written live to a `frontier_*.out` file in the working directory (path and timestamp echoed when the calculation was launched); it can be viewed with `tail -f` for full SCF details. Output JSON saved as formaldehyde_frontier_dft.json.

Computed results (selected):
• HOMO energy  –7.6523067 eV
• LUMO energy  –1.7826355 eV
• HOMO–LUMO gap  5.8696712 eV (Koopmans estimate: vertical gap)

All values are electronic (no zero-point or thermal corrections). No experimental comparison was requested.
